fields.label: Light Color Choice
fields.why: Some light spells let the caster choose the light color while others have a fixed or unspecified color.
fields.comment: Records whether the light color is chosen by the caster, fixed by the spell, or not mechanically specified. Purpose: Light Color Choice exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].light.colorChoice`. The value type is `enum`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.caster_choice: Use `caster_choice` for Light Color Choice only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.fixed: Use when Light Color Choice has one fixed canonical value and the caster does not choose from multiple values.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Light Color Choice only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: Light explicitly says the light can be colored as the caster likes.

fields.label: Light Opaque Cover Blocks
fields.why: Some object-originated light can be blocked by covering the source with something opaque.
fields.comment: Records whether opaque cover blocks the emitted light from the affected source. Purpose: Light Opaque Cover Blocks exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].light.opaqueCoverBlocks`. The value type is `boolean-or-not-applicable`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.true: Use when the canonical prose explicitly says the Light Opaque Cover Blocks mechanic is present or active for this spell.
acceptedValueNotes.false: Use when the canonical prose explicitly rules out the Light Opaque Cover Blocks mechanic or the absence matters mechanically.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Light Opaque Cover Blocks only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: Light and Daylight-style object lights can state how opaque cover interacts with the source.

fields.label: Sound Audible Radius
fields.why: Sound-producing spells with a gameplay-facing audible distance need an explicit radius instead of prose-only handling.
fields.comment: Records how far the spell's sound can be heard. Use `not_applicable` when the spell does not create a discrete audible event with a rules-facing distance. Purpose: Sound Audible Radius exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].soundEmission.audibleRadius`. The value type is `integer-or-not-applicable`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Sound Audible Radius only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: Thunderous Smite, Thunderwave, Knock, and Thunder Step all specify sounds audible at 300 feet.

fields.label: Sound Audible Radius Unit
fields.why: Audible radii need units so feet, miles, and absence do not share one string field.
fields.comment: Names the unit used by Sound Audible Radius. Use `not_applicable` when no sound radius is present. Purpose: Sound Audible Radius Unit exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].soundEmission.radiusUnit`. The value type is `enum`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.feet: Use when Sound Audible Radius Unit is measured in feet; keep the numeric distance in the paired value field.
acceptedValueNotes.miles: Use when Sound Audible Radius Unit is measured in miles; keep the numeric distance in the paired value field.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Sound Audible Radius Unit only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: Sound radius values need explicit distance units.

fields.label: Sound Source
fields.why: Sound can originate from the caster, a target object, a teleport origin, or an area, and the runtime needs that source.
fields.comment: Records where the audible event comes from so the runtime can measure the radius from the right origin. Purpose: Sound Source exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].soundEmission.source`. The value type is `enum`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.caster: Use when the canonical prose makes the Sound Source originate from the caster.
acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the canonical prose makes the Sound Source originate from the target.
acceptedValueNotes.target_object: Use `target_object` for Sound Source only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.origin_space: Use `origin_space` for Sound Source only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.spell_area: Use `spell_area` for Sound Source only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Sound Source only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: Knock's sound comes from the target object, Thunder Step's boom comes from the departure space, and Thunderwave's boom comes from the spell area.

fields.label: Sound Trigger

fields.comment: Records when the audible event happens relative to the spell or effect. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic, not when the mechanic is unknown or has not been reviewed.

acceptedValueNotes.on_cast: Use `on_cast` for Sound Trigger only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.on_hit: Use `on_hit` for Sound Trigger only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.after_teleport: Use `after_teleport` for Sound Trigger only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use for Sound Trigger only when the canonical spell prose does not include this mechanic. This is a deliberate schema-safe sentinel, not an unknown or unreviewed value.

log.why: The first sound-radius packet needs to distinguish on-cast booms, hit-triggered thunder, and post-teleport thunder.
